title: C2 process-exclusive canonical geometry authority — PR 1 (record + additive factory + transition warning)
intent: Operationalize the C2 geometry-origin keystone ruling (PROPOSED / ratification-ready) as additive code: canonical geometry authority is process-derived, not artifact-derived. Canonical body geometry exists only as the output of the approved canonical process following a governed approval event. This PR adds the CanonicalProcessApprovalRecord model, an additive process-approved canonical-reference factory, and a transition-mode validation WARNING (never RED) for legacy canonical references that lack process-approval metadata. It does NOT mark C2 closed, does NOT add a router path (PR 2), does NOT reconcile governance docs (PR 3), and does NOT flip warnings to strict RED (PR 4). Process id/version/approval-rule literals are PROPOSED placeholders awaiting repo-owner ratification.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) NEW canonical_geometry_process_approval.py: CanonicalProcessApprovalRecord (pydantic) with structural invariants — non-empty process id/version/event/rule/source id/provenance/inputs hashes; decision must be 'approve'; system: approvers rejected; source_authority_state may not be a format/route/storage token (dxf/svg/step/route/storage/filename/format/path); source role may not claim authority. A small APPROVED_CANONICAL_PROCESSES registry + process_covers_source_case() enforce that an uncovered case fails with 'process extension required' (never an artifact-specific exception). Factory create_canonical_process_approval_record() and validate_canonical_process_approval_record(). Proposed placeholder constants PROPOSED_CANONICAL_PROCESS_ID/_VERSION/_APPROVAL_RULE_ID flagged RATIFICATION PENDING. (2) geometry_authority_reference.py: added optional process-approval metadata fields (process_approval_record_id/_hash, canonical_process_id/_version, governed_approval_event_id, process_source_geometry_id); new create_process_approved_canonical_geometry_reference() factory; compute_hash() binds the approval record id/hash ONLY when present (legacy/derived hashes unchanged); legacy create_canonical_geometry_reference() docstring marked LEGACY/UNAPPROVED. (3) geometry_authority_validation.py: new validate_canonical_process_authority() helper; validate_geometry_authority_reference() now appends a transition-mode WARNING (not a blocking issue) when a canonical reference lacks process-approval metadata. (4) geometry_authority_taxonomy.py: canonical layer description changed from 'owned by IBG/BOE' to 'produced by the approved canonical process following a governed approval event'. (5) NEW test_canonical_geometry_process_approval.py: 34 tests covering the ruling. (6) test_geometry_authority_references.py: two existing tests re-pointed at the process-approved factory (a valid canonical reference is now a process-approved one → still green/pass) plus one added test documenting the legacy-warns-not-red transition.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: This is the first code expression of the C2 geometry-origin ruling; no existing primitive encodes process-derived authority. The legacy create_canonical_geometry_reference() grants canonical authority from origin alone, which is exactly what the ruling forbids — the new record/factory add the missing governed-approval binding, and the validation warning surfaces (without breaking) every legacy canonical reference so callers can migrate before a future strict RED flip. The taxonomy wording removes an IBG/BOE-ownership claim that contradicted the ruling. Existing 7T behavior (derived-source requirement, non-canonical authority-collapse RED gates, execution/machine-output invariants) is unchanged; the only behavior delta is the additive transition warning and the additive process-approved path.

commands_run: pytest tests/cam/test_geometry_authority_references.py -> passed (2 tests re-pointed to process-approved factory; +1 legacy-warns test)

pytest of §9 regression set (geometry authority + IBG intake/constitutional + DXF/IBG provenance guards) -> 176 passed, 2 failed; both failures (test_permissive_gate_tolerates_bypass_attempts, test_rejected_candidate_stays_blocked) confirmed PRE-EXISTING on main via git stash baseline run — zero regression from this PR
python -m app.ci.check_complexity --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json -> only violation is pre-existing untracked scripts/worktree_reaper.py (not in this PR's file set)

files.intent: New CanonicalProcessApprovalRecord model + factory/validation encoding process-exclusive canonical authority; proposed placeholder process vocabulary flagged for ratification
files.behavior_change: additive - new model/factory; no runtime export/solver/CAM behavior changed

files.intent: Additive process-approval metadata fields + create_process_approved_canonical_geometry_reference(); legacy factory docstring marked unapproved; hash binds approval only when present
files.behavior_change: additive - new fields default None; legacy/derived reference hashes preserved

files.intent: validate_canonical_process_authority() helper + transition-mode WARNING (not RED) for canonical references lacking process-approval metadata
files.behavior_change: additive-transition-warning - legacy canonical references now yellow-warn; no new RED gate

files.intent: Canonical layer description reworded from IBG/BOE-ownership to approved-canonical-process output
New test suite proving artifacts/formats/routes/registries/individual reviewers cannot self-create canonical authority
Re-point two existing canonical-green/pass tests at the process-approved factory; add one legacy-warns-not-red transition test
